reviewer.findings: C5 adds ntt_perf::bench_schoolbook_vs_ntt, a #[ignore]-by-default cycle-accurate (rdtsc, median-of-4000) micro-benchmark comparing schoolbook poly_mul vs poly_mul_ntt on identical random operands, with a falsifiable assertion that NTT must be strictly faster. Ran --release --ignored: schoolbook median 759022 cyc, NTT median 27456 cyc, 27.65x speedup, test passes. This is the last piece of the wire-in justification: C1-C3 established correctness/behaviour-neutrality and constant-time-ness, C5 establishes the wire-in actually delivers the speed it was done for. Full pq_kem suite: 14 passed, 2 ignored (both release/perf-only by design), 0 failed.

overlap.findings: Confirmed this benchmark measures exactly the operation C2 swapped (the ring multiply) and nothing broader, is deterministic (fixed xorshift seed) so the ratio is reproducible run-to-run modulo scheduler noise, and is appropriately #[ignore]d (not part of default cargo test, since perf numbers are environment-dependent and not a correctness gate). Confirmed no production code changed in this commit — pure addition of a #[cfg(test)] module. This closes out the crypto side of the plan (C1-C5); C6 (infra/gate-script fixes) is unrelated and kept separate.
